title: Garlic Crispy Sichuan Chicken Stir-fry
description: A garlicky, crispy chicken dish tossed in a creamy coconut sauce with classic Sichuan spices—perfect for a post-workout high-protein meal.
instructions: Toss chicken pieces in cornstarch until fully coated; shake off excess.
Heat 1 tbsp of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the coated chicken until golden and crispy, about 4-5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same wok, add the remaining oil and reduce to 1 tbsp. Sauté minced garlic and ginger for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add chili bean paste and Szechuan peppercorn powder; stir for another 30 seconds to bloom the spices.
Pour in coconut cream, soy sauce, and 2 tbsp water to thin the sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Let the sauce thicken, about 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Return the crispy chicken to the wok; toss to coat evenly with the sauce and cook for 2 more minutes to heat through.
Add sliced green onions and drizzle sesame oil, cooking for an additional 30 seconds.
Taste the sauce; adjust salt or soy sauce if needed.
Serve hot over cauliflower rice or steamed broccoli for a low-carb option. Enjoy!
